evals.prompt: Use Tailwind CSS in a Next.js application
evals.expected_output: Install tailwindcss + postcss + autoprefixer; configure tailwind.config.ts content paths; use cn() utility (clsx+tailwind-merge) for conditional classes; zero runtime, RSC compatible

evals.prompt: Set up shadcn/ui components with Next.js
evals.expected_output: npx shadcn-ui@latest init; components copied to components/ui/; extend theme in tailwind.config.ts with CSS variable colors; uses Radix UI primitives; customize via variants in component files

evals.prompt: Use clsx and tailwind-merge for conditional classes
evals.expected_output: import { clsx } from 'clsx'; import { twMerge } from 'tailwind-merge'; export function cn(...inputs) { return twMerge(clsx(inputs)); } usage: className={cn('base', isActive && 'bg-blue-500', className)}

evals.prompt: Add SCSS modules to a Next.js project
evals.expected_output: Create Component.module.scss; import styles from './Component.module.scss'; use className={styles.container}; install sass package; classes are auto-scoped; zero runtime overhead

evals.prompt: Integrate Ant Design with Next.js App Router
evals.expected_output: Install @ant-design/nextjs-registry; wrap in <AntdRegistry> Client Component in app/layout.tsx; prevents SSR flash; Ant Design components usable in Server and Client Components with proper wrapping

evals.prompt: Prevent runtime CSS from breaking Server Components in Next.js
evals.expected_output: Avoid MUI, Chakra UI, Emotion (runtime CSS-in-JS); they inject styles via JS at runtime which conflicts with RSC; use Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, or SCSS instead; all zero-runtime options

should_not_trigger: Tailwind CSS configuration in a Vue.js app
CSS-in-JS with emotion in a Gatsby site
Material UI setup in a Vite React SPA
